key_insight: Gravitational failures are best understood as a latent system state that evolves through damage, stress redistribution, and external forcing until catastrophic collapse occurs.
engineering_relevance: This framework helps engineers unify monitoring, modeling, and early-warning efforts by focusing on reconstructing a system’s evolving proximity to failure rather than hunting for universal precursors.
method: Model failure as progressive evolution of an internal latent state
Link damage accumulation, stress redistribution, and forcing across scales
Infer state from incomplete observations using physical and data-driven methods
evidence_signal: The abstract presents a conceptual synthesis rather than a new dataset or quantitative validation, emphasizing cross-disciplinary structure over measured performance.
watch_item: Its practical value depends on how reliably incomplete observations can be translated into the true evolving state, which may be difficult in heterogeneous natural systems.
paper_title: A Unified Conceptual Framework for Gravitational Instabilities: From Latent System State to Failure
